item.title:
Three months ago, Codex was trash for knowledge work. Now it's my daily driver.
I use it for writin...
item.description:
Three months ago, Codex was trash for knowledge work. Now it's my daily driver.
I use it for writing, recruiting, deep engineering work, and everything in between. It even keeps me at inbox 0.
I chatted with @every's head of growth Austin @tedescau on @every's AI & I about what changed, and why he now spends 80% of his working time in the Codex desktop app too.
We get into: - How Codex went from making Austin feel like an idiot to being the place he goes to get stuff done, including complex tasks like writing go-to-market plans using existing material from Slack, Notion, and meeting transcripts. - Why the Codex’s desktop app, which is faster and more reliable than Claude Desktop/Cowork, is the real differentiator. - How I source candidates with Codex by having it identify career arcs, not keywords—my go-to move is identifying organizations likely to teach the skills Every needs for a role, and then find candidates from that pool who have since gone on to work in AI.
This is a must-watch for anyone who's wondering whether it’s finally time to give Codex a try.
Watch below!
Timestamps How Codex went from a tool for senior engineers to a daily driver for knowledge work: 00:00:57 How Claude Code proved that a great coding agent works for any knowledge work: 00:02:42 Austin's switch to Codex: 00:07:24 How Austin set up Codex with folders, keys, and reviewer agents: 00:13:48 Using Codex to brainstorm automations across Gmail, Slack, and Notion: 00:18:24 How Austin manages the human review step when Codex is drafting communications: 00:22:42 Using Codex to build specialized agents inspired by product executive Claire Vo: 00:28:54 Synthesizing meeting transcripts and Slack threads into a go-to-market plan: 00:31:09 Building a live KPI tracker in Notion that agents can read: 00:40:15 Using Codex for recruiting: 00:44:54
--- @danshipper | ❤️ 11 | 🔁 0 | 💬 2 | 👁 604
item.author: @danshipper

item.description:
Exclusive fireside chat at Binance Online with Co-CEOs @heyibinance &amp; @_RichardTeng
They’ll discuss the journey from 300M → 3B users. Plus the infrastructure, products, and the sauce making that scale possible.
Save your seat now → https://t.co/vsjcfeIvjg https://t.co/yGgJ0pKAL0

item.author: @Space_Station

item.title:
Am I right that hyperscaling compute is the biggest bet in history?
Any counter examples?
It’s w...
item.description:
Am I right that hyperscaling compute is the biggest bet in history?
Any counter examples?
It’s way more expensive than the Manhattan Project, the Apollo project, and railways across the US.
If it doesn’t yield AGI, it may also be the biggest failed bet in history.
--- @GaryMarcus | ❤️ 75 | 🔁 5 | 💬 21 | 👁 2902
item.author: @GaryMarcus

item.title:
The shift is simple, but critical:
Move AI closer to where decisions happen.
With Edge Control fro...
item.description:
The shift is simple, but critical:
Move AI closer to where decisions happen.
With Edge Control from @TMobileBusiness: → Ultra-low latency through local data processing → Private-network-like performance with secure data handling → Reliable, near real-time responsiveness for mission-critical applications
All managed through T-Platform, a unified portal that provides a single place to monitor and control every Edge Control deployment at scale.
That’s how AI moves from analysis to execution.
Learn More: https://t.co/7BLgoYkULw
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 1 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 104
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Here’s the problem with most AI setups:
→ Data is sent to the cloud → Models process remotely → Dec...
item.description:
Here’s the problem with most AI setups:
→ Data is sent to the cloud → Models process remotely → Decisions come back with delay
That delay is latency.
And in operations, latency slows performance, limits responsiveness, and impacts business value.
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 3 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 144
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
In real operations, speed isn’t a feature. It’s the requirement.
Think about: → Manufacturing lines...
item.description:
In real operations, speed isn’t a feature. It’s the requirement.
Think about: → Manufacturing lines → Logistics networks → Smart infrastructure
These environments depend on ultra-low latency and real-time responsiveness.
They don’t need insights later. They need decisions now.
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 1 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 66
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Many AI projects don’t fail because of bad models.
They fail because of where the AI runs.
That’s ...
item.description:
Many AI projects don’t fail because of bad models.
They fail because of where the AI runs.
That’s the part most teams overlook, and why “working AI” in the lab doesn’t translate into real operations.
Here’s the breakdown…
@TMobileBusiness Partner https://t.co/gsCntYnBQW
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 9 | 🔁 1 | 💬 1 | 👁 249
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon
